stem: An engineer takes 15 measurements of body weight using a bathroom scale. The measurements have a mean of 136.5 kg and a standard deviation of 9.2 kg. However, calibration tests reveal that a bathroom scale systematically overestimates the true value by between 3.6% and 9.3%. Estimate the true body weight and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 136.5 kg. After correcting for the 6.5% bias: ≈ 128.2 kg. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (3.6%-9.3%) and sampling variability: [120.1, 136.6]. Point estimate: 128.2 kg.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
